original: 荒海や佐渡によこたふ天河
english: A rough sea; stretching out toward Sado, the Milky Way.
spanish: Mar bravo; extendiéndose hacia Sado, la Vía Láctea.

original: 旅に病んで夢は枯野をかけ廻る
english: Sick on a journey; my dreams wander over withered fields.
spanish: Enfermo en el viaje; mis sueños vagan por campos secos.

original: やれ打つな蠅が手をすり足をする
english: Do not swat the fly; it rubs its hands, it rubs its feet.
spanish: No golpees a la mosca; se frota las manos, se frota los pies.

original: いくたびも雪の深さを尋ねけり
english: Again and again I ask how deep the snow is.
spanish: Una y otra vez pregunto qué tan profunda está la nieve.

original: 五月雨や大河を前に家二軒
english: Summer rains; before the great river, two houses.
spanish: Lluvias de verano; ante el gran río, dos casas.

english: Have you eaten your rice gruel? Then wash your bowl.
spanish: ¿Ya comiste tus gachas de arroz? Entonces lava tu cuenco.

english: Gutei raised one finger.
spanish: Gutei levantó un dedo.

english: Ordinary mind is the Way.
spanish: La mente ordinaria es el Camino.

english: The heavens tell the glory of God.
spanish: Los cielos cuentan la gloria de Dios.
notes: Hebrew Bible source; compact working rendering.

english: Deep calls to deep.
spanish: Un abismo llama a otro abismo.
notes: Hebrew Bible source; compact working rendering.

english: Teach us to number our days.
spanish: Enséñanos a contar nuestros días.
notes: Hebrew Bible source; compact working rendering.

english: Night shines like day.
spanish: La noche resplandece como el día.
notes: Hebrew Bible source; compact working rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 11 — Use of Emptiness
original: 有之以為利，無之以為用
english: What is there gives benefit; what is not there gives use.
spanish: Lo que está da provecho; lo que no está da uso.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing
notes: Classical Chinese source; compact working rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 16 — Return to Root
english: Returning to the root is called stillness.
spanish: Volver a la raíz se llama quietud.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing
notes: Classical Chinese source; compact working rendering.

english: Returning is the movement of the Dao.
spanish: El retorno es el movimiento del Dao.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing
notes: Classical Chinese source; compact working rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 48 — Less and Less
english: In pursuing the Dao, less each day.
spanish: Al seguir el Dao, cada día menos.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing
notes: Classical Chinese source; compact working rendering.

english: Truth is one; the sages speak of it in many ways.
spanish: La verdad es una; los sabios la dicen de muchas maneras.

english: We meditate on the adorable radiance of Savitṛ.
spanish: Meditamos en el resplandor venerable de Savitṛ.

english: Then there was neither non-being nor being.
spanish: Entonces no había ni no-ser ni ser.

english: You are That.
spanish: Tú eres Eso.

english: I am Brahman.
spanish: Yo soy Brahman.

original: ¿Acaso de veras se vive con raíz en la tierra?
english: Do we truly live with roots in the earth?
spanish: ¿Acaso de veras se vive con raíz en la tierra?
recommendedEditionSource: University of Oklahoma Press — Fifteen Poets of the Aztec World
notes: Nahuatl poetic tradition via Spanish scholarly rendering.

original: No acabarán mis flores, no cesarán mis cantos.
english: My flowers will not end; my songs will not cease.
spanish: No acabarán mis flores, no cesarán mis cantos.
notes: Nahuatl manuscript tradition via Spanish rendering.

original: بشنو از نی چون حکایت می‌کند
english: Listen to the reed, how it tells a tale.
spanish: Escucha la caña, cómo cuenta su historia.
notes: Persian Sufi source; compact working rendering.

original: الا يا أيها الساقي ادر كأسا وناولها
english: O cupbearer, turn the cup and pass it around.
spanish: Oh copero, haz girar la copa y pásala.
notes: Persian ghazal tradition; compact working rendering.
